summary: The United Arab Emirates runs a National Post-Quantum Migration Programme under its Cyber Security Council, anchored by a National Encryption Policy that requires entities to hold officially approved plans to move from traditional encryption to post-quantum cryptography. The Council has deployed a national Crypto Discovery Tool with the Advanced Technology Research Council, and the Dubai Electronic Security Center has issued its own post-quantum cryptography guideline.
govActors:
UAE Cyber Security Council | Issues the National Encryption Policy and runs the National Post-Quantum Migration Programme and the national Crypto Discovery Tool
ATRC | Advanced Technology Research Council, partners on the national post-quantum security transition and the Crypto Discovery Tool deployment across critical infrastructure
DESC | Dubai Electronic Security Center, issued a Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline
provenance: summary + govActors (Cyber Security Council): National Encryption Policy mandating approved transition plans from traditional to post-quantum cryptography, backed by an executive regulation, and the National Post-Quantum Migration Programme and Crypto Discovery Tool announcements (included results rows, including WAM)

dominantProcess Sovereign: the UAE runs its own national programme - a National Encryption Policy, a National Cryptography Center setting requirements for the Crypto Discovery Tool, and a National Post-Quantum Migration Programme. No NIST FIPS suite is adopted as the named national standard in the included summaries; TII's submissions to NIST are research participation, not national adoption - REVIEW

govActors (ATRC): ATRC partnership announcements on the national post-quantum security transition and QuantumGate Crypto Discovery Tool deployment across critical national infrastructure (included results rows)
govActors (DESC): DESC Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline launched at GISEC Global 2025 (included results row)
hybridDeployment None stated: no included document states a hybrid position

standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED. TII contributed MiRitH, PERK and RYDE to the NIST additional-signatures call and released the first UAE PQC library, but these are research-capacity items, not a national adopted standard or algorithm set
migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: OMITTED, no dated milestones or completion date stated in the included documents
Model 2026-06-14 (NIST-bloc / taker, Medium): National Encryption Policy is a migration mandate over standardised algorithms, not an algorithm of its own. Reclassified from Sovereign.

mainRegulation:
National Encryption Policy and executive regulation (PQC transition) | national | binding-law
National Post-Quantum Migration Programme and Crypto Discovery Tool | national | guidance
obligation: Government entities must hold officially approved plans to transition from traditional encryption to post-quantum cryptography under the National Encryption Policy; the national programme provides discovery and migration tooling. The policy mandates migration, not a national algorithm.
